title: calendula suffruticosa subsp. monardii
intro: Calendula suffruticosa subsp. monardii, also known as the mountain marigold, is a perennial herb that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the mountainous regions of California and Oregon. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which can help soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The health benefits of Calendula suffruticosa subsp. monardii include its ability to promote skin health, reduce inflammation, and support wound healing, making it a valuable herb for treating minor cuts, burns, and skin irritations. Calendula suffruticosa subsp. monardii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote skin health and well-being.
